default: (a) a {@item component pouch|phb} or (b) an {@item arcane focus|phb}
(a) a {@item dungeoneer's pack|phb} or (b) an {@item explorer's pack|phb}

fluff.name: Wychlaran Witch
entries: The Witches of Rashemen, also known as Wychlaran, meaning "wise old women" in the ancient language of Halardrim, are the spiritual leaders of Rashemen, communing with the spirits and guiding the souls of the Rashemi people.
Wychlarans develop a bond with their land that gives them exceptional power. This power always stays with them, as long as they don't leave their homeland.

The formal term for a member of this group Is "hathran" ("learned sister"), while the most powerful and respected were called "othlor" ("true one"). In Rashemen, a member of the Wychlaran could do no wrong. Her word was law and to disobey it was punishable by death.

The Witches of Rashemen are based in Urlingwood, an area forbidden to any non-Wychlaran. Any trespassers found in the wood were killed on sight. The witches meet here to brew jhuild, as well as to bind and control hostile spirits and spy on Thayan forces.
They all wear masks to conceal their faces when traveling outside of Rashemen.
As a Wychlaran, you gain the following class features.
Every Witches of Rashemen use a very special staff: {@item Wychlaran Specialty Weapon|BWWW}.

classFeature.name: Ritual Caster
entries: At 1st level, the Wychlaran can cast spells as a ritual for those that have the 'ritual' tag and that they have prepared.

classFeature.name: Coven Casting
entries: At 1st level, the Wychlaran add {@spell Magic Circle|PHB} to your list of available spells.

Three or more Wychlarans can work together to cast spells as a Coven. The Coven Casting version of a spell takes a minimum of 10 minutes -OR- the specified casting time multiplied by the number of Wychlaran in the Coven (whichever is greater). The required spell slots are split evenly amongst the Coven, which means that a Coven of 3 could split a 9th level spell into three 3rd level spell slots (of which they must have available). At least one of the Wychlaran must have the spell prepared and known. In cases where the required spell slot cannot be evenly split, the lead witch consumes a higher level slot before any other witch in the Coven (minimum of 1).
All Coven Casting spells require Concentration. If any witch within the Coven Casting loses their concentration, then the spell fails and/or must be restarted.
Coven spells can be cast at a higher level, but at least 1 witch must have that level of spell slot available.
Coven Casting allows for multiple castings of higher level spells as long as no witches within the coven expend a 6th level or higher spell slot.
Cantrips are not eligible for Coven Casting.
In special cases (per the DM's discretion), a spell could be cast higher than level 9.

classFeature.name: Spirit Weaver
entries: At 1st level, You can cast {@spell Speak with Dead|PHB} as a Ritual. This spell does not count against your number of spells known.

classFeature.name: Hathran Site
entries: At 1st level, You can see normally in Darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 60 feet (You gain {@sense darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.).

classFeature.name: Commune with the Land
entries: At 1st level, You can cast {@spell Commune|PHB} without expending a spell slot (lvl 1-5: 1/day; lvl 6-8: 2/day; lvl 9+: 3/day)

classFeature.name: Wychlaran Domain Spells
entries: These spells are always prepared, but do not count towards your total number of prepared spells.

entries.caption: Wychlaran Domain Spells
rows: {@spell flame dagger|BWWW}, {@spell one with the land|BWWW}

classFeature.name: Fearsome
entries: At 3rd level, you can cast {@spell Fear|PHB} a number of times = your Proficiency level without using a Spell Slot (mana).
You gain Proficiency with Intimidation or Persuasion. If you are already proficient in either, then gain Expertise in that skill instead.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you cannot increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you cannot increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you cannot increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you cannot increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you cannot increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Spiritual Alignment
entries: At 4th level, you gain another Cantrip as a Domain Cantrip. The cantrip must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 8th level, you gain another 1st level Spell as a Domain Spell, The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 13th level, you gain another 2nd level Spell as a Domain Spell. The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 20th level, you gain another 3rd level Spell as a Domain Spell. The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
Domain spells do not count towards your number spells prepared and you always have them prepared.

classFeature.name: Spiritual Alignment
entries: At 4th level, you gain another Cantrip as a Domain Cantrip. The cantrip must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 8th level, you gain another 1st level Spell as a Domain Spell, The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 13th level, you gain another 2nd level Spell as a Domain Spell. The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 20th level, you gain another 3rd level Spell as a Domain Spell. The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
Domain spells do not count towards your number spells prepared and you always have them prepared.

classFeature.name: Spiritual Alignment
entries: At 4th level, you gain another Cantrip as a Domain Cantrip. The cantrip must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 8th level, you gain another 1st level Spell as a Domain Spell, The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 13th level, you gain another 2nd level Spell as a Domain Spell. The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 20th level, you gain another 3rd level Spell as a Domain Spell. The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
Domain spells do not count towards your number spells prepared and you always have them prepared.

classFeature.name: Spiritual Alignment
entries: At 4th level, you gain another Cantrip as a Domain Cantrip. The cantrip must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 8th level, you gain another 1st level Spell as a Domain Spell, The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 13th level, you gain another 2nd level Spell as a Domain Spell. The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
At 20th level, you gain another 3rd level Spell as a Domain Spell. The spell must be from the school of Necromancy or Abjuration.
Domain spells do not count towards your number spells prepared and you always have them prepared.

classFeature.name: The Three Provides
entries: At 5th lvl, a wychlaran no longer requires material components (except for spells that consume materials with a stated GP value; their required value is halved). The three gods are {@deity Chauntea}, {@deity Mielikki} and {@deity Mystra}.

classFeature.name: Telepathic Touch
entries: At 6th level, a wychlaran can Concentrate on a single target within 30 feet and use a Bonus Action to send a telepathic Message of 25 words or less. The Target can reply as a Free Action to do so while your focus on it continues. As long as your concentration is not broken and the range is not exceeded, then the communication link remains and you can continue to communicate each round.

classFeature.name: Spirit Sanctuary
entries: When you reach 14th level, creatures of the spiritual world sense your connection and become hesitant to attack you. When a celestial or undead creature attacks you, that creature must make a WIS save versus your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature must choose a different target, or the attack automatically misses. On a successful save, the creature is immune to this effect for 24 hours. The creature is aware of this effect before it makes an attack against you. The Sanctuary is broken if you attack or harm the creature.

classFeature.name: Witches Flight
entries: At 18th level, you gain a fly speed equal to your base speed. You also gain the ability to hover.

subclass.name: Duty Not Done
subclassFeatures: Duty Not Done|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|Duty Not Done|BWWW|1

subclass.name: Mischief Not Managed
subclassFeatures: Mischief Not Managed|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|Mischief Not Managed|BWWW|1
Beck And Call|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|Mischief Not Managed|BWWW|6
Dance Of The Departed|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|Mischief Not Managed|BWWW|6
The Show Goes On|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|Mischief Not Managed|BWWW|17

subclassFeature.name: Duty Not Done
entries: You strive to further a cause, uphold a virtue, or defend a person or place of sacred significance. Your uncanny magic protects and empowers your allies, hoping to do together what you couldn't alone.

subclassFeature.name: Augment
entries: When you choose this unfinished business at 1st level, and every time you gain a level in this class there after, you learn one spell of your choice from the {@filter Wychlaran Witch spell list|spells|class=Wychlaran Witch} (printed at the end of the class description).

items: A spell you choose must be of a level no higher than what's shown in the {@filter Wychlaran Witch table's|spells|class=Wychlaran Witch} {@i Supernatural Powers} column for your level.
You can only cast augment spells while possessing a host.
Augment spells with a range of self affect your host, not you. Otherwise, such spells target only your host regardless of their normal range.
Augment spells require no components, and all such spells end immediately if the possession ends.
You can cast a number of augment spells equal to your proficiency bonus.
You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

entries: Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the augment spells you know and replace it with another spell from the {@filter Wychlaran Witch spell list|spells|class=Wychlaran Witch}, which must also be of a level no greater than your {@i Supernatural Powers} value.

subclassFeature.name: Aegis
entries: Starting at 6th level, whenever you take the Embolden action, you grant temporary hit points equal to your ghost level (instead of your Charisma modifier).
Additionally, if your host is reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, they immediately become stable. The possession then ends as normal.

subclassFeature.name: Otherworldly Guardian
entries: Starting at 11th level, a creature possessed by you is always under the effects of a {@spell protection from evil and good|PHB} spell.

subclassFeature.name: Unyielding Protector
entries: Starting at 17th level, your concentration on augment spells cannot be broken as a result of your host taking damage.
Additionally, whenever you roll for initiative, you regain one expended use of your Augment feature.

subclassFeature.name: Malice Unsated
entries: Your appetite for vengeance has gone unsatisfied, and you strive to bring harm to a hated foe, wreak havoc on an organization that wronged you, or repay a transgression in kind. You use hosts like puppets to achieve your goals, unable to pursue your agenda without mortal aid. Your {@i supernatural powers} can turn foes against one another and reanimate the fallen as pawns in your loathsome game.

subclassFeature.name: Malefic Possession
entries: Starting when you choose this unfinished business at 1st level, you can use your {@classFeature Possession|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|1|BWWW} ability on unwilling creatures. An unwilling target can be of any creature type except undead and may make a Charisma saving throw to resist the effect. If it passes, the possession fails, and you remain wherever you were before the attempt.
An unwilling host gains none of the benefits of your {@classFeature Possession|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|1|BWWW} ability. You gain the following action options while possessing an unwilling host:

items: {@i The Gibbering}: As an action, you force your host to make a Constitution saving throw. If they fail, they lose their concentration on spells, and can speak only in gibberish until the start of your next turn.
{@i Maddening Whispers}: As an action, you force your host to make a Wisdom saving throw. If they fail, they have disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.

{@i Wrest Control}: As an action, you force your host to make a Charisma saving throw. If they fail, they behave as if affected by a confusion spell until the end of their next turn. You can't use Wrest Control again before the possession ends.

entries: An unwilling host can use its action to make a Charisma save. If it succeeds, the possession ends. The possession also ends if the host travels to a different plane of existence, leaving you behind. Once an unwilling possession ends, or after a target succeeds on the initial saving throw, you must finish a long rest before you can possess that creature again.
When an unwilling possession ends, you can use your reaction to possess a willing humanoid within 30 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: Life Drain
entries: Starting at 1st level, when a creature you're possessing hits with a melee attack, you can cause the attack to deal additional necrotic damage. The extra damage is a number of d8 equal to your {@i Supernatural Powers} value, as shown in the {@filter Wychlaran Witch table|spells|class=Wychlaran Witch}. A creature dealt damage this way can't regain hit points until the end of its next turn.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Grim Puppeteer
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you can possess the corpses of the slain. While not possessing a host, you can use your action to disappear into an intact corpse you can see within 5 feet of you. The corpse must be Large or smaller and be that of a beast or a humanoid. You are now effectively the possessed creature, which gains none of the benefits of your {@classFeature Possession|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|1|BWWW} ability. Its type becomes undead, though it now looks alive, and it gains a number of temporary hit points equal to your hit point maximum.

While possessing a corpse, you maintain your alignment, Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, and immunity to poison. You otherwise use the possessed target's game statistics, gaining access to its proficiencies, but not its class features, if any.

The possession lasts until the temporary hit points are lost (at which point the body becomes a corpse once more) or you end it as a bonus action. When the possession ends, you reappear in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the corpse.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Domineering Will
entries: By 11th level, you've grown strong enough to seize full control of unwilling hosts. While possessing an unwilling beast or humanoid, you gain the following action option:

items: {@i Subjugate}: As an action, you force your host to make a Charisma saving throw. If they succeed, the possession ends. If they fail, they are incapacitated, and you assume complete control of their body until the possession ends. On your turn, you may forgo your own movement and actions to manipulate the host. When you do, you can immediately make it move and perform actions appropriate to its game statistics. You don't gain access to the target's knowledge, nor class features, if any.

entries: Once a creature fails its saving throw against this effect, you must finish a long rest before you can use {@i Subjugate} again.

subclassFeature.name: Undying Hate
entries: Beginning at 17th level, whenever you roll for initiative, you regain all expended uses of your Life Drain feature.

Additionally, any creature dealt damage by your Life Drain has its hit point maximum reduced by the same amount. The reduction lasts until the creature finishes a long rest. The target dies if this reduces its hit point maximum to 0.

subclassFeature.name: Mischief Not Managed
entries: Not all who wander between worlds are grim spectres and dour phantoms. You linger to experience a thrill, pull off a heist, or entertain a loved one you couldn't while you were alive. Armed with a sense of humour uncommon amongst the dead, you regard your allies and hosts as accomplices in your supernatural pranks.

entries.subclassFeature: Soul Of Discretion|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|Mischief Not Managed|BWWW|1

subclassFeature.name: Soul Of Discretion
entries: When you choose this unfinished business at 1st level, you gain proficiency in the Stealth skill.

subclassFeature.name: Poltergeist
entries: Starting at 1st level, you can cast {@spell catapult|XGE} and {@spell mage hand|PHB} at will, requiring no components. When cast in this way, the hand produced by {@i mage hand|PHB} is {@condition invisible}, and the damage dice of catapult are replaced with a number of {@dice 1d8} equal to your {@i Supernatural Powers} value, as shown in the {@filter Wychlaran Witch table|spells|class=Wychlaran Witch}.

subclassFeature.name: Beck And Call
entries: At 6th level, you gain proficiency in the Sleight of Hand skill (see {@classFeature Possession|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|1|BWWW} for the benefits of proficiency).
Additionally, when you cast mage hand, you can perform the following additional tasks with it:

items: You can stow one object the hand is holding in a container worn or carried by another creature.
You can retrieve an object in a container worn or carried by another creature.

entries: You can perform one of these tasks without being noticed by a creature if you succeed on a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check contested by the creature's Wisdom (Perception) check.

subclassFeature.name: Dance Of The Departed
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you can cast {@spell animate objects|PHB}, requiring no components. When cast in this way, you can animate a number of objects equal to your {@i Supernatural Powers} value (instead of ten), as shown in the Wychlaran Witch table.
Once you cast {@spell animate objects|PHB} in this way, you must finish a short or long rest before you can do so again.

subclassFeature.name: Subtle Possession
entries: Beginning at 11th level, you can use your {@classFeature Possession|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|1|BWWW} ability on unwilling creatures. An unwilling target can be of any creature type except undead and may make a Charisma saving throw to resist the effect. If it passes, the possession fails, and you remain wherever you were before the attempt. Pass or fail, the target must succeed at a Wisdom (Insight) check against your Wychlaran Witch save DC to determine that anything is amiss.
An unwilling host gains none of the benefits of your {@classFeature Possession|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|1|BWWW} ability. You gain the following action options while possessing an unwilling host:

items: {@i Delude}: You can cast phantasmal force, requiring no components. When cast in this way, the spell targets only your host, and ends early if the possession ends. You can't use Delude again before the possession ends.

{@i The Gibbering}: As an action, you force your host to make a Constitution saving throw. If they fail, they lose their concentration on spells, and can speak only in gibberish until the start of your next turn.
{@i Steal Thoughts}: You can cast detect thoughts, requiring no components. When cast in this way, the spell detects only your host, and ends early if the possession ends.

entries: An unsuspecting host can use its action to repeat the Insight check, deducing your presence on a success. The host becomes aware of you automatically if you force them to make a saving throw. An unwilling host that learns of your presence can use its action to make a Charisma save. If it succeeds, the possession ends. The possession also ends if the host travels to a different plane of existence, leaving you behind.

Once an unwilling possession ends, or after a target succeeds on the initial saving throw, you must finish a long rest before you can possess that creature again. When an unwilling possession ends, you can use your reaction to possess a willing humanoid within 30 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: The Show Goes On
entries: Starting at 17th level, whenever you roll for initiative, if your {@subclassFeature Dance Of The Departed|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|Mischief Not Managed|BWWW|6} feature has been expended, you immediately regain its use.
Additionally, you can cast telekinesis, requiring no components. Once you cast the spell in this way, you must finish a short or long rest before you can do so again.

subclassFeature.name: Restless Blade
entries: For some, death on the battlefield is the highest honor or final insult, for others, it is the first step on a path of endless conflict. You aim to train a successor, perfect a martial art, or overcome a monster that you couldn't in life, but your skills go to waste without hands that can wield a blade. You look to your allies and hosts to pursue your goals, sharing in their glory as you guide and protect them.

subclassFeature.name: One With Blade
entries: When you choose this unfinished business at 1st level, you gain proficiency with simple and martial weapons (see {@classFeature Possession|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|1|BWWW} for the benefits of weapon proficiency).

Additionally, whenever you take the {@i Harmonize} action, the attack you grant advantage to deals additional damage. The extra damage is a number of {@dice 1d8} equal to your {@i Supernatural Powers} value, as shown in the Wychlaran Witch table. The damage type is the same as the attack's.

subclassFeature.name: Ephemeral Shield
entries: Starting at 1st level, when a creature you're possessing suffers damage that is less than your current hit points, you can take the damage instead by absorbing the blow into your spectral form. The host's resistances and immunities apply as usual, but the damage you take can't be reduced in any way. Other effects inflicted by the damage's source apply to your host as normal.

subclassFeature.name: Phantasmal Warrior
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you've learned to wreathe yourself in energy, allowing you to impact the world around you for a limited time. Doing so requires a bonus action, and immediately ends your possession if you're possessing a host.

For one minute, you gain temporary hit points equal to your hit point maximum. While you have these hit points, you can make attacks, and can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. While any of these hit points remain, your AC can't be less than 16, and you can interact with objects and equipment normally.

Your unarmed strikes in this state deal force damage, representing phantasmal blades, swiping claws, or other elements of your spectral appearance. In place of the normal damage die of your unarmed strike, you can roll a number of d6 equal to your Supernatural Powers value, as shown in the Wychlaran Witch table.
After one minute, or when the last of these temporary hit points are lost but you are still conscious, you can use your reaction to possess a willing humanoid within 30 feet of you.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Otherworldly Champion
entries: Starting at 11th level, you can extend your phantasmal presence to surround your host, lending strength to their strikes and cladding them in your crackling aura like armor.

While possessing a creature, you can cast {@spell Tenser's transformation|XGE}, requiring no components. When cast in this way, the spell affects your host, not you, and ends early if the possession ends. Once you cast Tenser's transformation in this way, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

subclassFeature.name: Deathless Defiance
entries: By 17th level, nothing can be done to stay your immortal wrath. Whenever you roll for initiative, if your {@subclassFeature Phantasmal Warrior|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|Restless Blade|BWWW|6} feature has been expended, you immediately regain its use.
Additionally, while in your phantasmal state, you are immune to the {@condition charmed} and {@condition frightened} conditions.

optionalfeature.name: Chill Of The Grave

otherSummary.entry: {@subclassFeature Malice Unsated|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|Malice Unsated|BWWW|1|} unfinished business
{@subclassFeature Mischief Not Managed|Wychlaran Witch|BWWW|Mischief Not Managed|BWWW|1|} unfinished business

entries: While possessing a creature, you can cast {@spell hold monster|PHB}, targeting only your host. When cast in this way, the spell requires no components, and ends early if the possession ends.
Once you cast {@spell hold monster|PHB} in this way, you must finish a short or long rest before you can do so again.

optionalfeature.name: Cloak Of Sorrows
entries: If a creature hits your host with a melee attack while your host has temporary hit points from your {@i Embolden} action, the creature takes a number of {@damage 1d6} psychic damage equal to your {@i Supernatural Powers} value, as shown in the Wychlaran Witch table.

optionalfeature.name: Wychlaran Witchly Flicker
entries: While possessing a host, you can cast {@spell blade ward|PHB} at will, requiring no components. When cast in this way, the spell affects your host, not you, and ends early if the possession ends.

optionalfeature.name: Haunting Guise
entries: As an action, you can momentarily take on a horrifying appearance. Each non-undead creature within 30 feet of you that can see you or a creature you're possessing must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you for 1 minute. A {@condition frightened} target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, with disadvantage if they are within 30 feet of you, ending the effect on itself on a success. Your host, if any, is immune to this effect, and affected creatures treat them as the source of their fear.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

optionalfeature.name: Keening Wail
entries: As an action, you can release a haunting moan. Creatures within 10 feet of you that can hear you must make a Constitution saving throw. A creature that fails takes {@damage 2d6} psychic damage and has disadvantage on saving throws until the end of your next turn. Your host, if any, is unaffected.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you roll for initiative.

optionalfeature.name: Knot In The Weave
entries: While possessing a spellcaster, you can use your action to replenish some of their spent spell slots. When you do, your host can choose spell slots to recover with a combined level equal to or less than half your ghost level (rounded up). None of the recovered slots can be 6th level or higher. Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.
For example, if you're a 4th level ghost, your host can recover up to two levels' worth of spell slots. They can recover either a 2nd-level spell slot or two 1st-level spell slots.

optionalfeature.name: Knowledge From Beyond
entries: You can cast {@spell divination|PHB}, requiring no components, gleaning answers from the spirits of the beyond. After casting the spell in this way, you can choose one skill, tool, or language. For the next 10 minutes, you and any creature you're possessing have proficiency with the chosen skill, tool, or language.
Once you cast divination in this way, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

optionalfeature.name: Knowledge From Within
entries: One foot in the otherworld lets you draw on experience far beyond your years. You may add your {@i Charisma modifier to the result of any Arcana, Nature, History, or Religion} check you make.

optionalfeature.name: One With The Mist
entries: While possessing a creature, you can cast {@spell invisibility|PHB} or {@spell gaseous form|PHB} targeting only your host. These spells end early if the possession ends. Alternatively, while not possessing a creature, you can cast {@spell misty step|PHB}. When cast in this way, these spells require no components.
After using this manifestation once, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

optionalfeature.name: Otherworldly Spark
entries: While possessing a willing creature, your host's attacks with nonmagical weapons are considered magical.
Additionally, you can cast {@spell elemental weapon|PHB}, requiring no components, targeting one weapon worn or carried by your host. The spell ends early if the possession ends.
Once you cast {@spell elemental weapon|PHB} in this way, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

optionalfeature.name: Phantasmal Strike
entries: Be it with spectral blades, grasping tendrils, or raking claws, you've mastered {@filter manifestations|optionalfeatures|feature type=OF:BWWW} with which to assault your foes. You can cast {@spell spiritual weapon|PHB} at will, requiring no components, and can attack with it despite your Spirit Form.

optionalfeature.name: Spectral Affinity
entries: You can spend 1 hour drawing a magic item within 5 feet of you into the Border Ethereal. The item then disappears or is subsumed into your spectral form.

Thereafter, you are considered to be wielding, wearing, or carrying the item as appropriate. If eligible, you can attune to the item while this process occurs. You can't affect an artifact or sentient item this way, and you can be bound to no more than 3 magic items at a time. The item appears at your feet if you die, or if you use an action to break your bond with it.

optionalfeature.name: Spirit Sight
entries: You can cast {@spell comprehend languages|PHB}, {@spell detect evil and good|PHB}, {@spell detect magic|PHB}, {@spell locate object|PHB}, and {@spell see invisibility|PHB}, requiring no components. You can do so a total number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
While possessing a creature, you share the benefits granted by these spells with your host.

optionalfeature.name: Tear In The Veil
entries: You can cast {@spell etherealness|PHB}, requiring no components, targeting yourself, your host (if any), and up to three willing creatures within 10 feet of you.
Once you cast {@spell etherealness|PHB} in this way, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

optionalfeature.name: Voice Of The Departed
entries: While not possessing a creature, you can cast {@spell speak with dead|PHB} at will. When cast in this way, the spell requires no material component, and has a range of touch.
Additionally, you automatically know when the corpse tells a lie (though you don't have the power to compel it to tell the truth).

item.name: Wychlaran Specialty Weapon
item.reqAttune: by a sorcerer, warlock, wizard or Wychlaran Witch
entries: At 3rd level a Wychlaran constructs a staff made of wood harvested from the Urlingwood. The staff is then named by its owner (i.e. Urling Ice Staff, Urling Staff of Force, Radiant Urling Staff, Night-Tree Staff, etc)

entries.name: Staff Form
entries: While in 'Staff' form, it acts as a 1-handed bludgeoning weapon for 1d6 + WIS mod damage OR as a 2-handed bludgeoning weapons for {@damage 1d8} + WIS mod damage. (see below for types, but each Wychlaran chooses one type)

entries.name: Spells
entries: Also, the staff can store up to 5 charges (spell slots/mana). The Wychlaran can cast any number of spells into the staff that total up to 5 spell slots / mana (from herself or another spellcaster; similar to a Ring of Spell Storing). She can then expend a number of charges equal to the level of the spell to cast that spell as an Action.

entries.name: Whip Form
entries: As a bonus action, the wielder can change the form of the staff into a whip with a reach of 15 ft.

items: While in the 'whip' form, it crackles with elemental energy
On a hit, the whip does {@damage 1d4} slashing damage, plus {@damage 1d4} elemental (of Wychlaran's choice: Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, Poison or Radiant).
The base damage increases to {@damage 1d6} at 6th level, {@damage 2d4} at 9th level, {@damage 1d10} at 12th level, {@damage 2d6} at 15th level and {@damage 2d8} at 18th level
Alternatively, in place of the extra elemental damage, the whip can latch onto a target that it hits and force it to become restrained (no save).
Alternatively, the Wychlaran can cast the '{@spell Rope Trick|PHB}' spell using the whip instead of a rope

spell.name: Nature Watch
entries: Using the Hathran sight granted by the powers of The Three, you can determine the condition of creatures and plants near death within the spell's range. You instantly know whether each creature/plant within the area is dead, fragile (alive and wounded, or {@condition unconscious}), fighting off death (making death saves), undead, or neither alive nor dead (such as a construct). Naturewatch sees through any spell or ability that allows creatures to feign death.

spell.name: Scatterspray
entries: Base on: {@spell Catapult|XGE}

You Choose any number of objects whose total weight is between 1 and 5 pounds within range that is not being worn or carried. The objects fly in a straight line up to 90 feet in a direction you choose before falling to the ground, stopping early if they impact against a solid surface. If the objects would strike a creature, that creature must make a DEX save vs {@damage 3d8} bludgeoning damage.

spell.name: Flame Dagger

components.m: a lit candle or a piece of flint

entries: {@b Duration:} 1 minute per Wychlaran level.

A 1-foot long blazing beam of red-hot flame springs forth from your hand. You wield this blade as if it was a dagger. Attacks with the blade deal Fire damage equal to {@dice 1d4} + Proficiency Bonus + Spell Casting Ability Modifier. The Flame Dagger can ignite combustible material that is not being worn or carried. Does NOT function under water.

spell.name: One with the land
entries: {@b Duration:} 1 hour per Wychlaran level.
You cast the spell and are bathed in a golden-green light from above. As it fades, you feel more in touch with your natural surroundings.
You forge a strong link with nature that gives you Advantage on Animal Handling, Stealth, Nature and Survival checks for the duration.

spell.name: Flash Burst
entries: A blinding flash of light erupts from a target you choose. Creatures within a 20 foot radius area must make a CON save vs your spell save DC. On a fail, they become blind & {@condition stunned}. On a success, they have disadvantage on their first attack roll. A creature can reroll the save at the end of each of their turns. On a success, they are no longer blind or {@condition stunned}.

spell.name: Moon Blade
entries: {@b Duration:} 1 minute per Wychlaran level.

A 3-foot long blazing beam of moonlight springs forth from your hand. The sword deals {@dice 1d8} plus your spell casting modifier of Radiant Damage. Undead receive an additional {@dice 1d8} of radiant damage. In addition, any caster struck by the Moon Blade had disadvantage on its CON save to maintain concentration.

spell.name: Land Womb
entries: {@b Target:} You and one other creature.

You descend into a protective bubble within the earth below you. Spectators around you see the earth open up around you and swallow you as you descend into the earth. You cannot enter the Land Womb through stone or rock, only through tillable soil. The land womb holds you secure 10 feet below the surface. Anyone within the Land Womb cannot be perceived by divination magic of 4th level or lower. You may bring one additional medium creature that you are touching with you into the Land Womb. You can breathe and speak within the Land Womb, but you cannot hear or see anything on the surface without magical means. You can end the spell at-will, which returns all within the Land Womb to the surface from which it was originally cast.

spell.name: Lesser Planar Ally
entries: By casting this spell, you request The Three (Chauntea, Mielikki and Mystra) to send you an elemental or undead spirit (of 6 HD or less) of The Three's choice. If you know an individual creature's name, you may request that individual by speaking the name during the spell (though you might get a different creature anyway). You may ask the creature to perform one task in exchange for a payment from you. You must be able to communicate with the creature in order to bargain for its services. Payment must be agreed upon and made before the creature agrees to perform any services. At the end of the task (or when the agreed upon time frame expires), the creature returns to its home plane (after reporting to you, if appropriate and possible).

spell.name: Lesser Planar Binding
entries: Casting this spell attempts a dangerous act: to lure a creature from another plane to a specifically prepared trap, which must lie within the spell's range. The called creature is held in the trap until it agrees to perform one service in return for its freedom.

The 'Trap' must be a Magic Circle focused inward. The kind of creature must be known and stated. If you wish to call a specific individual, you must use that individual's proper name in casting the spell. The target creature is allowed a WIS save. If the save succeeds, the creature resists the spell. If the saving throw fails, the creature is immediately drawn to the trap (spell resistance does not keep it from being called).

The creature can escape the trap with successful WIS save, by dimensional travel, or with a successful CHA check versus your spellcasting save DC (each of these attempts are limited to 1/day). (HOMEBREW Note: Wychlaran's can ask for an undead spirit (of equal power). Rashemen's lore has more of an undead spirit flavor.)

spell.name: Moon Path
entries: Moon Path allows you to make a stair or bridge from one spot to another. The effect is like a railless ribbon of glowing white, translucent force like a glass strip. The strip can be from 3 to 20 feet wide. You can designate a number of creatures equal to your Proficiency bonus that receive the Sanctuary spell as they cross the strip. A Moon Path must be straight, continuous and unbroken when formed. Certain spells/effects can destroy part of the path, but the path remains in place, even without supporting structures underneath.

spell.name: Planar Ally (Wychlaran Witch)
entries: You beseech an otherworldly entity for aid. The being must be known to you: a god, a primordial, a demon prince, or some other being of cosmic power. That entity sends a {@filter undead|bestiary|type=undead|miscellaneous=!swarm} loyal to it to aid you, making the creature appear in an unoccupied space within range. If you know a specific creature's name, you can speak that name when you cast this spell to request that creature, though you might get a different creature anyway (DM's choice).

When the creature appears, it is under no compulsion to behave in any particular way. You can ask the creature to perform a service in exchange for payment, but it isn't obliged to do so. The requested task could range from simple (fly us across the chasm, or help us fight a battle) to complex (spy on our enemies, or protect us during our foray into the dungeon). You must be able to communicate with the creature to bargain for its services.

Payment can take a variety of forms. A undead might require a sizable donation of gold or magic items to an allied temple, while a fiend might demand a living sacrifice or a gift of treasure. Some creatures might exchange their service for a quest undertaken by you.

As a rule of thumb, a task that can be measured in minutes requires a payment worth 100 gp per minute. A task measured in hours requires 1,000 gp per hour. And a task measured in days (up to 10 days) requires 10,000 gp per day. The DM can adjust these payments based on the circumstances under which you cast the spell. If the task is aligned with the creature's ethos, the payment might be halved or even waived. Nonhazardous tasks typically require only half the suggested payment, while especially dangerous tasks might require a greater gift. Creatures rarely accept tasks that seem suicidal.

After the creature completes the task, or when the agreed-upon duration of service expires, the creature returns to its home plane after reporting back to you, if appropriate to the task and if possible. If you are unable to agree on a price for the creature's service, the creature immediately returns to its home plane.
A creature enlisted to join your group counts as a member of it, receiving a full share of experience points awarded.

spell.name: Planar Binding (Wychlaran Witch)

m.text: a jewel worth at least 1,000 gp, which the spell consumes

entries: With this spell, you attempt to bind a {@filter undead|bestiary|type=undead|miscellaneous=!swarm} to your service. The creature must be within range for the entire casting of the spell. (Typically, the creature is first summoned into the center of an inverted {@spell magic circle} in order to keep it trapped while this spell is cast.) At the completion of the casting, the target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, it is bound to serve you for the duration. If the creature was summoned or created by another spell, that spell's duration is extended to match the duration of this spell.

A bound creature must follow your instructions to the best of its ability. You might command the creature to accompany you on an adventure, to guard a location, or to deliver a message. The creature obeys the letter of your instructions, but if the creature is hostile to you, it strives to twist your words to achieve its own objectives. If the creature carries out your instructions completely before the spell ends, it travels to you to report this fact if you are on the same plane of existence. If you are on a different plane of existence, it returns to the place where you bound it and remains there until the spell ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of a higher level, the duration increases to 10 days with a 6th-level slot, to 30 days with a 7th-level slot, to 180 days with an 8th-level slot, and to a year and a day with a 9th-level spell slot.

spell.name: Greater Planar Binding (Wychlaran Witch)

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of a higher level, the duration increases to 10 days with a 6th-level slot, to 30 days with a 7th-level slot, to 180 days with an 8th-level slot, and to a year and a day with a 9th-level spell slot.
{@b Targets:} Up to three elementals or outsiders, totaling no more than 18 HD, no two of which can be more than 30 ft. apart when they appear.

Casting this spell attempts a dangerous act: to lure a creature {@filter undead|bestiary|type=undead|miscellaneous=!swarm} from another plane to a specifically prepared trap, which must lie within the spell's range. The called creature is held in the trap until it agrees to perform one service in return for its freedom.

To create the trap, you must use a Magic Circle spell, focused inward. The kind of creature to be bound must be known and stated. If you wish to call a specific individual, you must use that individual's proper name in casting the spell.

The target creature is allowed a Will saving throw. If the saving throw succeeds, the creature resists the spell. If the saving throw fails, the creature is immediately drawn to the trap (spell resistance does not keep it from being called). The creature can escape from the trap with by successfully pitting its spell resistance against your caster level check, by dimensional travel, or with a successful Charisma check (DC 15 + 1/2 your caster level + your Cha modifier). It can try each method once per day. If it breaks loose, it can flee or attack you. A dimensional anchor cast on the creature prevents its escape via dimensional travel. You can also employ a calling diagram (see {@spell magic circle} against evil) to make the trap more secure.

If the creature does not break free of the trap, you can keep it bound for as long as you dare. You can attempt to compel the creature to perform a service by describing the service and perhaps offering some sort of reward. You make a Charisma check opposed by the creature's Charisma check. The check is assigned a bonus of +0 to +6 based on the nature of the service and the reward. If the creature wins the opposed check, it refuses service. New offers, bribes, and the like can be made or the old ones reoffered every 24 hours. This process can be repeated until the creature promises to serve, until it breaks free, or until you decide to get rid of it by means of some other spell. Impossible demands or unreasonable commands are never agreed to. If you roll a 1 on the Charisma check, the creature breaks free of the binding and can escape or attack you.
Each creature gets a saving throw, makes independent attempts to escape, and must be persuaded to aid you individually.

Once the requested service is completed, the creature need only so inform you to be instantly sent back whence it came. The creature might later seek revenge. If you assign some open-ended task that the creature cannot complete though its own actions the spell remains in effect for a maximum of one day per caster level, and the creature gains an immediate chance to break free. Note that a clever recipient can subvert some instructions.
Note: When you use a calling spell such as gate to call an air, chaotic, earth, evil, fire, good, lawful, or water creature, it is a spell of that type.
